---
title: "Housing Data Modeling"
subtitle: "Unit 4: Machine Learning - Classification & Regression"
author: "David Martinez"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
---

## Summary

This project applies both classification and regression techniques on housing data to predict property age and build year.

### Classification

Built a Random Forest Classifier to predict whether a house was built before 1980, achieving over 93% accuracy.

### Feature Engineering & Interpretation

Extracted feature importances to determine the strongest predictors of a home's age (living area, 1-story architectural style, and number of bathrooms). Plotted these relationships using boxplots and scatter plots.

### Regression (Stretch)

Combined original housing data with a neighborhood dataset. Built a Random Forest Regressor to predict the exact year a house was built, evaluating the model using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), R-squared metrics, and residual plots.
